title: Micron Technology Q1 FY 2026 Sets Records; Strong Q2 Outlook
text: Micron Technology delivered exceptional Q1 FY 2026 performance, with revenue reaching $13.6 billion, surpassing Wall Street's $13.0 billion consensus and representing 57% year-over-year growth. The company reported record results across all business segments, driven primarily by accelerating artificial intelligence infrastructure demand. Key financial highlights included Cloud Memory revenue of $5.3 billion (up 100% YoY) and Non-GAAP operating margins expanding to 47.0% from 27.5% in the prior year. CEO Sanjay Mehrotra stated: Our Q2 outlook reflects substantial records across revenue, gross margin, EPS, and free cash flow. The analysis highlights several critical dynamics supporting future growth. High-bandwidth memory supply remains fully committed through 2026, with Micron projecting the HBM market will expand from $35 billion in 2025 to approximately $100 billion by 2028. DRAM pricing improved roughly 20% sequentially amid tight industry inventories, while the company's 1-gamma DRAM node will comprise the majority of output by mid-2026. Beyond data centers, Micron demonstrated momentum across mobile, automotive, and client segments, with flagship smartphone models adopting higher memory configurations and automotive applications expanding through advanced driver assistance systems. Management raised capital expenditure plans to $20.0 billion, prioritizing high-bandwidth memory capacity and advanced node production across facilities in the United States, Japan, and Singapore.
